resources.string:
• First check Templates – there might already be a schedule that fits your needs.
• If not, set the cycle start date.
• Define cycle labels (e.g. A, B or Morning, Afternoon, Night).
• Select which label applies on the start date.
• Configure rules (skip weekends or holidays if needed).
• Save your settings.
• Optionally add the widget to your home screen.

• The start date is the reference point of the cycle.
• The selected label applies to that date.
• Each valid day advances the cycle.
• Skipped weekends or holidays do not affect the cycle.

• Assignments add extra labels (e.g. O1, vacation, sick leave).
• Edit a day in the 7-day preview or calendar.
• Assignments can override or complement the cycle.
• You can toggle assignment visibility in settings.

• Shows the next 7 days based on current settings.
• Tap a day to edit its assignment.
• Changes appear after saving.
• Reflects both cycle rules and assignments.

• Displays a monthly overview of the cycle.
• Each day shows a short label (A, B, O1).
• Tap a day to see details.
• Combines cycle and assignment data.

• The widget shows your current cycle.
• It updates automatically every day.
• You can customize its appearance in settings.
• Uses the same data as the app.

• Use short labels for better readability.
• Keep the cycle simple (A/B or 2–3 shifts).
• Use assignments for exceptions.
• If something looks wrong, check start date and rules.

• Added Work Log settings for daily target, breaks, overtime and expected work times
• Added expected start and end time per cycle label
• Added compact deviation tracking with late start, early finish and overtime status
• Added proper overnight and night-shift handling across midnight
• Added session snapshot so active work sessions keep their original cycle and expected times
• Added slider-based Start / Finish / End Break flow for safer actions
• Improved Work Log dashboard layout, clarity and active session overview
• Fixed multiple Work Log issues, notification safety and cleanup of old legacy code
